Marcus (brother, lives in Denver); they are close but haven't seen each other in over a year, currently only phone contact; Dana wants to change this in 2025
Wants to see brother Marcus in person this year after over a year of phone-only contact

items.text: Lost her mom recently (before 2025-02-03); described it as making this 'a heavy winter' and said it 'colors everything'; did not want to discuss details in this session
Priya (closest friend from grad school; ceramicist and freelance designer); they signed up for a Tuesday night pottery class together starting in February 2025
items.archivedReason: Both items describe the same relationship with Priya; merging captures the full arc from pottery class friendship through the loan conflict in one coherent entry

items.text: [as of 2025-02-12] Taking a Tuesday night pottery class with friend Priya; described as 'the first standing thing we've had on the calendar in years'

[2025-02-12] Mom had an old wooden sailboat, a 22-footer called the Dorothy Jean — named after Dana's grandmother. Mom taught Dana to sail on that boat. The boat has been sitting under a tarp in Priya's dad's barn out near Scappoose since mom got sick. On Sunday 2025-02-09, Dana went out, pulled the tarp off, and started restoration work (sanded one plank). Dana said: 'I've decided I'm going to restore her. Slowly. No deadline.' And: 'working with my hands on something of hers felt like the first right thing in months.'
[as of 2025-02-12] Restoring mom's sailboat (the Dorothy Jean) at a barn near Scappoose; working slowly with no deadline, described as therapeutic

supersededBy.text: Restoring mom's sailboat (the Dorothy Jean) at a barn near Scappoose; working slowly with no deadline; as of 2025-05-11, starboard side is sanded and has started re-caulking the hull seams

items.text: [2025-03-09] Marcus called Dana from the hospital hallway at approximately 2am Denver time after Nina's birth just to say 'she's here.' Dana said: 'We haven't talked like that — voice shaking, no agenda — in years. Denver suddenly feels way too far away.'

items.text: [2025-03-14] Dana scattered Mom's ashes at Beacon Rock on March 14. The urn had been in her closet for months and she couldn't look at that side of the closet. She drove out to the Gorge alone on a clear, cold Friday morning before she could talk herself out of it. Beacon Rock was theirs — Mom used to take Dana there as a kid, and on the way up taught her to read cloud shapes: 'Not just "that one's a rabbit" — she had this whole taxonomy, what a mare's tail sky meant, when the anvil clouds meant get off the water.' Dana stood at the top, the sky doing 'exactly the kind of high thin herringbone she loved,' and let her go into the wind. On the way down Dana picked up a fir cone from the trail; it's on the windowsill above her kitchen sink now. She drove home, Sam had soup waiting, and she slept eleven hours. (2025-03-18)

[as of 2025-04-01] Sam received a job offer as Perception team lead at a robotics startup in Austin, Texas; described as 'genuinely his dream problem space' with a strong offer; decision deadline early May 2025; Dana and Sam are considering whether to relocate together

supersededBy.text: Sam accepted the Perception team lead role at the Austin robotics startup with a remote work arrangement from Portland; starts June 2025; will visit Austin approximately one week every couple months; Dana described the negotiation as 'he gave something up too — the in-person team, the new city adventure' and said she believes the trade is good for him 'about eighty-five percent'

items.text: Facing decision about potential move to Austin for Sam's job offer; torn between supporting Sam's career opportunity and staying in Portland where 'this geography is load-bearing' for healing after mom's death; specifically concerned about leaving the Dorothy Jean restoration, Priya, pottery class, and the Gorge/Beacon Rock; Sam has said he won't take the offer 'if it costs us this year of me healing'; decision needed by early May 2025 (opened 2025-04-01)

supersededBy.text: Sam negotiated remote work from Portland with the Austin robotics startup; he accepted the role with arrangement to work remotely and visit Austin one week every couple months; starts June 2025; Dana and Sam are staying in Portland; Dana described feeling relief at not having to leave 'the load-bearing geography' this year

items.archivedReason: Austin move decision thread superseded by cs-37 which confirms the resolution (staying in Portland)

items.text: Priya: Approximately two weeks before 2025-04-27, Priya asked Dana to borrow eight thousand dollars for a ceramics studio lease deposit and buildout; Dana said no immediately and 'badly,' citing not mixing money and friendship; Priya went 'very quiet and then very polite'; they have skipped pottery class twice since; Dana described the exchange as delivering 'ten years of friendship a verdict in four seconds' and recognizes the delivery hurt more than the answer itself

She said: 'I hadn't let myself feel how scared I was of leaving this year — the barn, the boat, Beacon Rock, all of it. And I'd been quietly rehearsing how to be graceful about going. Not needing the rehearsal anymore was the relief.'

items.text: [2025-06-21] Marcus is flying out for the Dorothy Jean's first sail the weekend of July 12, bringing Nina (who will be four months old). When Dana told him the date, Marcus said 'Mom would have required my attendance' and booked flights that night. Dana said: 'Nina will be four months old. She obviously won't remember it, but there's going to be a photo of her in a tiny life jacket on our mother's boat, and I need that photo to exist.' Dana also said: 'I keep thinking about the cloud thing — I want to check the sky that morning the way she taught me. If it's a mare's tail sky I'm taking it as a blessing.' (2025-06-21)

[as of 2025-06-21] First sail of the Dorothy Jean scheduled for the weekend of 2025-07-12 at Cathedral Park; Marcus flying out with Nina for the occasion; Sam will be aboard; plan is a gentle afternoon sail on the river; Dana described feeling 'nervous in a good way' and said 'the point isn't the sailing' but rather who's aboard and whose boat it is
items.archivedReason: Plan for Dorothy Jean first sail superseded by sal-51 which confirms the sail happened

items.text: [2025-07-19] The Dorothy Jean sailed on July 12, 2025, exactly as planned — gentle afternoon on the Columbia with Sam working the lines, Marcus and Nina aboard. There's a photo of Nina asleep in a tiny life jacket against Marcus's chest with the mainsail up behind them. Dana said: 'I have already had it printed twice.' The sky was 'high thin stuff, not quite a mare's tail but close,' and Dana decided that counted as a blessing.

[as of 2025-07-19] Interoperability platform shipped in early July 2025 after eighteen months of development and internal politics; CEO gave Dana a shout-out by name in the all-hands; described as 'the biggest professional moment of my life'

Has historically defined herself through work ambition and achievement; described herself as 'the person who is her work' for fifteen years, 'not in a grim way — I mean it like an engine'; said 'ambition's been my whole spine' and 'that's how I've always known I'm okay'; as of 2025-07-19, experiencing unexpected emotional flatness at major professional wins, which she attributes to grief

[as of 2025-08-02] Interoperability platform launch in early July 2025 has led to ongoing production incident and high-pressure executive management; Dana described work as 'a pressure cooker since the launch' with an exec treating her as 'single point of contact at all hours'; experiencing stress affecting mood at home

supersededBy.text: [as of 2025-11-08] Work stress from interoperability platform launch in early July 2025 resolved; Dana described the period as 'a pressure cooker' that affected mood at home and caused her to 'snap at' Sam during fall 2025; stress resolved after reducing to four-day work week effective 2025-11-01

items.text: [as of 2025-08-16] VP Elaine offered Dana a formal director promotion on Thursday (approximately 2025-08-14) to head a merged product org with doubled org chart; Dana asked for time to think and declined over the weekend, citing bad timing due to interoperability platform's fragile stabilization phase and ongoing incident escalation responsibilities; Elaine indicated the door remains open and Dana could raise her hand again in spring 2026

[as of 2025-09-06] Enrolled in a twelve-week Saturday intensive ceramics class (wheel throwing) at a studio in Southeast Portland; three hours every Saturday morning; started first Saturday of September 2025; described the class as 'the first thing in months that felt like me' and said 'three hours where nobody needs an escalation decision and the clay does not care that I'm a product manager'
[as of 2025-09-06] Interoperability platform production incident finally closed as of early September 2025; work described as 'still a grind' but incident pressure resolved

Sprained ankle badly on Saturday 2025-04-13 during mile nine of a trail run in Forest Park (wet root); diagnosed as bad grade-two sprain; wore walking boot until Thursday 2025-05-01; cleared by physical therapist for light jogging (twenty minutes, flat ground, every other day) as of 2025-05-24; progressive recovery: thirty minutes by 2025-06-08, forty minutes by 2025-06-21, forty-five minutes by 2025-09-06

items.text: Finds deep satisfaction in hands-on creative work that quiets mental noise; described hours spent on Dorothy Jean restoration as 'the only hours all spring where my brain went quiet' and ceramics class similarly as making 'my own head quiet'; distinguished this from work achievements or obligation-driven activities

[2025-09-20] Priya received the arts-infrastructure grant for her ceramics studio buildout; the full amount was awarded, covering deposit and buildout; she signs the lease next month. Dana and Priya built the application together at Priya's kitchen table in June 2025. When Priya called with the news, both cried; Dana said: 'I don't think I've ever been so happy about something that has nothing to do with my own life.' Priya asked Dana if she wants a shelf in the new studio for Dana's ceramics work; Dana said yes immediately. Dana described this as 'medicine' after 'the four-second-verdict spring.' (2025-09-20)
[as of 2025-09-20] Nina (Marcus and Elena's daughter, born 2025-03-06) is crawling as of September 2025; Marcus sends videos frequently; Dana described her as 'a menace' affectionately

[as of 2025-09-20] Dana and Sam took the Dorothy Jean out twice more during summer 2025 after the July 12 launch; described the summer sailing as 'lovely quiet' and 'nothing dramatic, exactly the point'

[as of 2025-09-20] Sam's remote work arrangement with Austin robotics startup is working well; his team likes him; he is flying to Austin the week after 2025-09-20 for his regular every-couple-months in-person visit

[as of 2025-10-25] Reduced work schedule to four days per week (Fridays off) with twenty percent pay cut, effective 2025-11-01; HR processed 2025-10-24; told VP the reason was to 'protect stabilization bandwidth' but actual motivation is to spend Fridays restoring Priya's dad's barn in Scappoose (the barn where the Dorothy Jean was stored) and tending mom's garden tools and roses at the old house before it sells

[2025-10-25] Dana reduced to four days per week at work, Fridays off, effective November 1, 2025. She is spending Fridays restoring Priya's dad's barn in Scappoose — re-siding the north wall, fixing the roofline, building a real workbench — and putting Mom's garden tools back in there 'where they belong instead of in my basement.' She's also tending the roses at Mom's old house before it sells. Dana said: 'The days I spend at the wheel or in that barn are the days I feel like a person, and the days I spend making roadmap tradeoffs are the days I feel like a very competent function. And I'm forty-one and I lost my mom this year and I'm no longer willing to spend five out of seven days being a function. That's the real reason. It's not burnout — the work's fine, I'm good at it. It's that it stopped being the point.' (2025-10-25)

Has come to distinguish between feeling 'like a person' (days spent at the pottery wheel or working in the barn) versus feeling 'like a very competent function' (days spent making roadmap tradeoffs at work); no longer willing to spend five out of seven days being a function; described work as having 'stopped being the point' — not burnout, but a fundamental shift in what matters

[as of 2025-11-08] Work stress from interoperability platform launch in early July 2025 resolved; Dana described the period as 'a pressure cooker' that affected mood at home and caused her to 'snap at' Sam during fall 2025; stress resolved after reducing to four-day work week effective 2025-11-01

[as of 2025-11-08] First Friday off (2025-11-01) under new four-day work schedule; spent weekend of 2025-11-01 to 2025-11-03 at cabin near Mt Hood with Sam; described as 'the good kind of ordinary' with hiking to frozen-edged lake, gin rummy, cooking, reading; Sam observed 'I like you at four days a week already, and it's been a week' and noted Dana is 'lighter'; Dana described herself as 'easier to live with when I'm not spending myself down to the studs at the office'

entries.note: Pottery class with Priya has been skipped twice in the two weeks before 2025-04-27 due to conflict over loan request; Dana described the class as 'just sitting there on the calendar mocking me'
As of 2025-05-11, Dana still has not visited Denver to meet niece Nina (now two months old); Dana said this 'is starting to itch at me' — suggests growing urgency but no concrete plan yet

Session 2025-08-02: Dana snapped at Sam over a dinner question during high work stress week (production incident, exec pressure post-launch); apologized next morning; Sam responded 'you're carrying a lot, but I'm not the incident'; Dana described it as 'one bad night' and emphasized 'six years and we don't do the snipe-at-each-other thing'; relationship repair was immediate and complete; Dana's concern is about work stress affecting her behavior, not relationship health

Dana deferred a director promotion she described as 'the rung I've been climbing toward for years' and 'the obvious next move' — said six months ago she would have accepted immediately but now finds the prospect 'exhausting rather than exciting'; attributes this to 'grief-brain' and the cumulative weight of the year (mom, ankle, Austin uncertainty); explicitly frames this as potentially temporary ('ask me in the spring') rather than a fundamental shift in ambition

Session 2025-10-04: Q4 planning described as 'bloodbath' with Dana staying online until eleven most nights; work stress manifesting as irritability toward Sam; Dana described herself as 'mad at the laptop' and said 'it's my relationship to the job' that's cracking, not her relationship with Sam; pattern suggests ongoing tension between work identity and personal wellbeing

Session 2025-10-04: Dana snapped at Sam during Q4 planning stress ('must be nice to have a job you can close the laptop on'), apologized within the hour; described this as second such incident in recent months, pattern of 'work-stress acid leaking onto the nearest person'; both Dana and Sam identified the issue as Dana's relationship to work rather than relationship strain; Dana committed to protecting nine o'clock boundary; described relationship as 'better than we've been in years' outside work stress
